symptoms.symptom_name: Dull, heavy, constant right-upper-quadrant ache, chronic and worse over 48 hours
symptoms.character: dull, heavy, non-colicky, poorly localised fullness/pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Capsular stretch from hepatomegaly produces exactly this dull, non-colicky quality.
RUQ location overlaps, but the dull non-colicky character is atypical for gallbladder pain.
Fatigue is common in NAFLD/metabolic syndrome but is highly non-specific.
Central adiposity and insulin resistance are the core drivers of hepatic steatosis.
T2DM is strongly associated with hepatic steatosis and with progression to steatohepatitis/fibrosis and HCC.
Part of the metabolic cluster that drives fat deposition in the liver.
Minimal alcohol intake makes alcohol-related steatosis unlikely and supports a metabolic (non-alcoholic) aetiology.
Absence of fever makes cholangitis and acute cholecystitis less likely, but does not exclude early disease.
Systemic inflammatory features are usually present in established cholecystitis.
No jaundice lowers the probability of biliary obstruction/cholangitis, but patient self-inspection can miss subtle icterus — confirmation needs an in-person look and bloods.

symptoms.symptom_name: No vomiting and no sudden sharp/colicky pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Biliary colic/cholecystitis typically causes sharp, severe, often post-prandial pain with vomiting — absent here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth, firm, NON-tender hepatomegaly with a liver edge ~4 cm below the costal margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Smooth, non-tender hepatomegaly is typical of steatotic liver enlargement causing capsular stretch.
A smooth regular edge argues against the hard, irregular, nodular liver of malignancy — but this is a clinician palpation finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain.
A negative Murphy's sign reduces the likelihood of acute cholecystitis — but it is a hands-on examination finding sealed from the telehealth AI.
A cutaneous marker of marked insulin resistance, reinforcing the metabolic driver of steatosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spider naevi, palmar erythema or ascites

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of chronic-liver-disease stigmata argues against advanced cirrhosis — again a clinician examination finding not available remotely.
Raised blood pressure is part of the metabolic-syndrome cluster; the clinician-measured vital sign is sealed and not a telehealth input.

investigation_details.expected_result: Increased hepatic echogenicity ('bright liver') consistent with steatosis; assesses for gallstones, biliary dilatation and focal lesions
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Steatosis remains unconfirmed and gallstones, biliary obstruction or a hepatic mass could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bright liver on ultrasound is the first-line imaging confirmation of steatosis; anticipated but NOT available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Liver function tests / GGT / FBC / CRP / lipase

investigation_details.expected_result: Assesses hepatocellular vs cholestatic pattern, inflammation and pancreatic involvement; helps distinguish steatosis from cholecystitis/cholangitis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An inflammatory/cholestatic biliary process could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory markers and a cholestatic LFT pattern are needed to exclude biliary sepsis — unobtainable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Central obesity + T2DM + dyslipidaemia + acanthosis nigricans together give a strong pre-test probability of fatty liver as the cause of hepatomegaly and capsular-stretch pain.

No fever, no jaundice, no vomiting/colicky pain lowers the probability of an acute biliary emergency — but on history alone, not by exam/bloods/imaging, so it downgrades urgency without excluding the diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person abdominal examination (liver, gallbladder, Murphy's sign)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single highest-yield step the AI cannot perform — needed to characterise the liver edge, tenderness and Murphy's sign and to exclude an acute surgical abdomen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Liver function tests, GGT, FBC, CRP, lipase, fasting glucose/HbA1c, lipids
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguishes a hepatocellular (steatosis) from a cholestatic/inflammatory (biliary) picture and stages metabolic control.
First-line imaging to confirm steatosis and exclude gallstones, biliary dilatation and a focal hepatic mass.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT abdomen as the first-line test
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Ultrasound, not CT, is the appropriate first-line imaging for RUQ pain and suspected steatosis/biliary disease; CT is not the initial telehealth-arranged investigation.
